overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. LinkedIn has the largest follower base, while Bluesky shows the most recent activity. Overall engagement is moderate, with LinkedIn posts generally receiving more likes and comments compared to other platforms.

contentThemes.description: Research related to social media platforms, disinformation, and information operations.
Detection and analysis of coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media.
Application of computational methods to study social phenomena.
Posts related to hiring and research opportunities within the team.
Announcements and mentions of presentations at academic conferences.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to match activity on LinkedIn and Bluesky.
Utilize platform-specific content formats (e.g., longer-form articles on LinkedIn, shorter updates on Bluesky/Mastodon) to maximize engagement.
Engage more actively with followers by responding to comments and reposts, fostering a sense of community.
Use relevant hashtags consistently across all platforms to increase visibility and reach new audiences.
Consider running polls or Q&A sessions on Bluesky or Mastodon to boost interaction.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows active engagement with the research community, announcements of publications, and conference participation. Strong repost rate suggests content is shareable.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon posts focus on technical updates and discussions, but engagement (likes, reposts) is relatively lower compared to other platforms. May benefit from diversifying content.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the highest follower count and engagement, with posts related to grants, job opportunities, and research announcements performing well. Professional audience is receptive to this content.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
